title: Analytics Master EA
subtitle: Continuous Market Metrics, Fibonacci S/R, ATR Volatility, Account Info and Lot Sizing
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: BUILD — but split into components, not one monolithic EA

series_evolution.part_1: Script — visual ghost overlay, runs once
series_evolution.part_2: Script — text data panel, runs once
series_evolution.part_3: EA — same data panel but now runs continuously, adds ATR, account info, Fibonacci S/R, lot sizing, 2-hour auto-refresh
series_evolution.key_transition: Part 3 is the first LIVE tool in the series. It introduced the concept that the dashboard should refresh automatically — not just on demand. This is the architectural moment where we stop thinking in single-shot scripts and start thinking in event-driven services.

tool_classification.fixed_timeframe: D1 for price data, H1 for ATR

depends_on.note: Part 3 is a superset of Part 2 — it adds 5 entirely new data streams and a continuous update mechanism

new_data_introduced.name: atr_value
new_data_introduced.purpose: Measure intraday volatility — used in lot sizing and market assessment
new_data_introduced.note: H1 ATR captures hourly price range. More responsive than D1 ATR for intraday trading.

new_data_introduced.name: account_balance
new_data_introduced.purpose: Base for lot size calculation and risk management

new_data_introduced.name: account_equity
new_data_introduced.purpose: Real-time account value including open P&L — important for drawdown monitoring

new_data_introduced.name: market_spread
new_data_introduced.purpose: Transaction cost visibility — wide spreads make small trades uneconomical

new_data_introduced.name: min_lot_size
new_data_introduced.purpose: Broker minimum — lot size calculation must respect this floor

new_data_introduced.name: max_lot_size
new_data_introduced.purpose: Broker maximum — displayed for reference, lot calculation capped here

new_data_introduced.name: possible_lot_size
new_data_introduced.purpose: Suggested position size based on risk%, stop loss distance, and account balance

risk_percentage.description: % of account balance to risk per trade. Used in lot size calculation.

stop_loss_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to S/R distance when computing stop loss. 1.0 = full range.

atr_period.description: Number of H1 bars used in ATR volatility calculation.

update_interval_hours.description: How often to recalculate and redisplay all metrics.

lifecycle.OnInit: Initialise lastUpdateTime = 0. All data fetched on first tick.
lifecycle.OnTick: Every price tick calls UpdateMetrics(). UpdateMetrics only recalculates if 2 hours have elapsed since last run.
lifecycle.OnDeinit: Delete all chart objects on EA removal.

steps.name: time_gate_check
steps.description: Only proceed if (current_time - last_update_time) >= 7200 seconds. Prevents recalculation on every tick.
steps.our_improvement: Replace tick-based time gate with a proper scheduler that fires on bar close or configurable interval.

steps.name: fetch_daily_data
steps.description: Fetch prev day and today's D1 OHLCV from DailyContextProvider. Same as Parts 1 and 2.

steps.name: calculate_fibonacci_support_resistance
steps.description: NEW in Part 3 — extends S/R BEYOND the previous day's range using 0.382 Fibonacci ratio.
steps.vs_part_2: Part 2 set support=prev_low, resistance=prev_high (raw levels). Part 3 PROJECTS beyond those levels — the S/R is now a Fibonacci extension, not the raw H/L. These are completely different things and our build must expose this as a config choice.
steps.note: 0.382 is the first major Fibonacci retracement level. Extending the range by 38.2% gives projected support/resistance where price might find its next equilibrium.

steps.name: analyze_market_direction
steps.description: Close vs open (Bullish/Bearish/Neutral) PLUS wick pressure context

logic.base: close > open → Bullish | close < open → Bearish | equal → Neutral
logic.pressure_addon: high > open AND high > close → append 'with bullish pressure' | low < open AND low < close → append 'with bearish pressure'

steps.improvement_vs_part_2: Adds contextual wick analysis. A Bullish candle with a high wick (high above both open and close) suggests buying pressure exhausted — subtle but useful nuance.
steps.flaw: Still magnitude-blind. 'Bullish with bullish pressure' tells you nothing about range size.

steps.name: calculate_lot_size
steps.description: Risk-based position sizing using account balance, risk%, and S/R distance as stop loss proxy
steps.floor: If calculated lot < min_lot_size, use min_lot_size
steps.critical_flaw: Using the Fibonacci S/R distance as the stop loss distance is dangerous — these levels are projected beyond the actual range, so the stop loss distance will be LARGER than the actual daily range, producing SMALLER lot sizes than expected. The formula is internally consistent but the inputs are not what a trader would naturally use.

steps.name: fetch_account_metrics
steps.description: Get live account balance and equity from broker

steps.name: fetch_market_metrics
steps.description: Get live bid, ask, spread, and lot limits from broker symbol info

steps.name: calculate_atr_volatility
steps.description: Compute ATR(14) on H1 timeframe to measure intraday volatility
steps.note: ATR is the Average True Range — the average of (high-low, abs(high-prev_close), abs(low-prev_close)) over 14 H1 bars. Higher = more volatile session.

steps.name: render_data_table_and_lines
steps.description: Format all 17 metrics into the on-chart panel. Delete old trend lines and redraw at current prices.

part_3.meaning: Projected levels BEYOND the range — where price might go next

sr_strategy_comparison.our_build: Expose BOTH as config options. raw_hl=True for Part 2 behaviour, fib_extension=True for Part 3 behaviour. Default: raw_hl. This is a meaningful trading choice — raw H/L for conservative, Fibonacci extension for aggressive projection.

components_to_build.name: ATRCalculator
components_to_build.note: Pure function. Takes OHLC bars, returns ATR value. No broker dependency — input is just price data.

components_to_build.name: AccountDataProvider
components_to_build.contract: get_account() -> {balance: float, equity: float, currency: str}
components_to_build.note: Broker-specific. Each broker adapter implements this. For backtesting, returns simulated values.

components_to_build.name: MarketDataProvider
components_to_build.contract: get_market_info(symbol) -> {bid, ask, spread, min_lot, max_lot, tick_size, tick_value}
components_to_build.note: Live data — refreshed on each call. Broker-specific implementation.

components_to_build.name: LotSizeCalculator
components_to_build.contract: calculate(balance, risk_pct, stop_loss_distance, tick_size, min_lot) -> float
components_to_build.note: Pure function. No broker dependency. Unit testable. The most reused component in the toolkit — appears in almost every EA part.

components_to_build.name: FibonacciSRCalculator
components_to_build.note: Exposes both Part 2 (raw) and Part 3 (extension) behaviours via mode parameter.

components_to_build.name: ScheduledRefreshManager
components_to_build.our_improvement: Also support bar_close trigger as an alternative to time-based gate.

keep: The Script → EA evolution concept — live refresh is architecturally correct
ATR as volatility measure — standard, well-understood, reused in many future parts
Fibonacci S/R extension — more nuanced than raw H/L, worth exposing as an option
Account balance and equity in the dashboard — essential for live trading context
Market spread visibility — real transaction cost transparency
Lot size calculation structure — risk% × balance ÷ stop distance is industry standard
The 17-metric dashboard format — comprehensive daily briefing, good UX
AnalyzeMarketDirection with wick pressure context — improvement over Part 2
discard: Tick-based time gate (OnTick with 2-hour check) — inefficient, fires on every tick just to check a clock
ObjectsDeleteAll(0) on every refresh — deletes ALL chart objects including ones from other tools
DrawContinuousTrendLine using OBJ_TREND — MT5 specific, replaced by ChartRenderer
All MQL5 data APIs (iTime, iOpen, iHigh, iLow, iClose, iVolume, iATR, AccountInfoDouble etc.)
Hard-coding 0.382 — expose as config param (some traders prefer 0.236, 0.5, 0.618)
improve: Replace tick-gate with proper bar-close event or configurable interval scheduler
Namespaced object deletion — only delete objects created by this tool, not all chart objects
Make Fibonacci ratio configurable — default 0.382 but allow 0.236, 0.5, 0.618, 1.0
Add equity-to-balance ratio — if equity < 95% of balance, warn about open drawdown
Add spread warning threshold — flag when spread exceeds X% of ATR (unusually wide)
Volume normalisation (same fix as Part 2 — time-normalise current day volume)
ATR: expose timeframe and period as config, default H1/14 but allow D1/14 or H4/14

article_figures.title: Data Presentation (Part 2 recap)
article_figures.insight: Shows the Part 2 output to contrast with what Part 3 adds. Same data panel structure but Part 3 adds 10 more metrics.

article_figures.title: Analytics Comment Result (Part 2 recap)
article_figures.insight: Part 2's chart with S/R lines. Used as a before/after baseline — Part 3 generates the same lines plus the extra metrics.

article_figures.title: Volatility Formula
article_figures.insight: Standard deviation formula shown. The EA simplifies this by using ATR as a volatility proxy — ATR is more practical for trading than statistical standard deviation.

article_figures.title: Market Spread Formula
article_figures.insight: Spread = Ask - Bid. Simple but important. Confirms the spread is calculated live from market data, not estimated.

article_figures.title: Analytics Master EA (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Animated attachment of the EA to chart. Shows the full 17-metric panel appearing in the top-left. The red and blue trend lines are visible at prev day High and Low.

article_figures.title: Analytics Result with Timestamps
article_figures.insight: Static screenshot showing the full panel with Last Update Time stamps visible. Confirms the 2-hour refresh cycle is working correctly — two different timestamps shown in sequence.

important_notes_from_comments: Tool does NOT open trades — it is analysis only. Confirmed by the author in forum reply.
Updates every 2 hours — direction signal can flip between bullish/bearish on each refresh.
Audible alerts not implemented — suggested by a user as a valuable feature to add.
MT4 version does not exist and will not be made — MT4 is discontinued.
